claims.claim: Jülich AI inference tender bundles PDUs into a single rack-infrastructure line item alongside CDUs, IT hardware, and software rather than as a discrete operator-selected purchase
claims.sourceTitle: Jarvis Eine Zentrale Plattform Für Ki Inferenz... Germany Tender
claims.excerpt: Rack infrastructure, including power distribution units (Pdu) and cooling distribution units (Cdu) optimized for the offered it equipment.

claims.claim: Estimated value of the Jülich JARVIS AI-inference procurement bundling PDU/CDU with IT hardware
claims.sourceTitle: Germania Jülich JARVIS eine zentrale Plattform für KI Inferenz
claims.excerpt: Valoare estimata : 23,100,000 EUR | Tip anunt: UE | ID: 11093637 | Data publicarii : 01.07.2026

claims.claim: Schneider Electric and Foxconn are merging rack integration capability with power/cooling systems into jointly produced hardware for AI data centres
claims.sourceTitle: Schneider Electric and Foxconn to Partner on AI Data Centres | Data Centre Magazine
claims.excerpt: The collaboration merges Foxconn's global manufacturing capacity and advanced AI rack integration capabilities with Schneider Electric's power, cooling and energy management systems.

claims.claim: Schneider-Foxconn partnership aims to co-develop reference architectures and modular power/cooling skids, with production beginning later in 2026
claims.sourceTitle: Schneider Electric & Foxconn team up on AI data centres
claims.excerpt: The arrangement centres on co-developing reference architectures for AI data centres, pointing to a more standardised approach as operators face rising demand for computing capacity.

claims.claim: Neocloud operator Iren signed a $1.6bn contract with Dell for air-cooled Blackwell systems, an integrator/OEM deal rather than an operator-direct component tender
claims.sourceTitle: Iren taps Dell Technologies for Nvidia Blackwell servers under $1.6bn contract - DCD
claims.excerpt: AI cloud firm Iren has signed a $1.6bn contract with Dell to acquire air-cooled Blackwell systems.

claims.claim: Wiwynn (an ODM) identifies liquid cooling and power delivery, not GPU supply, as the current AI infrastructure bottleneck, with pressures possibly extending to 2028
claims.sourceTitle: Wiwynn AI & Data Center Energy 2026, $4.75B Ecolab Deal - EnkiAI
claims.excerpt: Server original design manufacturer (ODM) Wiwynn has identified emerging bottlenecks in liquid cooling and power delivery systems, warning that these supply pressures could extend to 2028

claims.claim: AI server rack power density now regularly exceeds 100kW, described as a more than 10x increase
claims.sourceTitle: Wiwynn AI & Data Center Energy 2026, $4.75B Ecolab Deal - EnkiAI
claims.excerpt: The power density of AI server racks now regularly exceeds 100 k W, a more than 10 x inc

claims.claim: One supplier's report attributes 30% of its 2025 orders to data centers, cited as qualitative channel context rather than a buyer-side direct-purchase share
claims.sourceTitle: Data center growth driving power equipment sales
claims.excerpt: One leading supplier reported data centers as its biggest growth driver, accounting for 30% of orders in 2025 — qualitative channel context only.

tensions.text: The node's derivation asserts 'large operators buy direct' to justify a 0.40 direct-purchase share, but the tender and partnership evidence actually found (Jülich JARVIS tender bundling PDU/CDU into one IT-equipment line item; Schneider-Foxconn co-developing integrated rack/power/cooling hardware; Iren's $1.6bn all-in Dell contract) shows PDU spend being procured as embedded integrator/OEM packages, not as operator-selected discrete lines.

The evidence attached to the node (Alexander Group: 30% of one supplier's 2025 orders were data centers) measures a supplier's order composition, not the buyer-side channel split (direct vs. integrator-embedded) that the 0.40 estimate purports to represent — a mismatch between cited evidence and claimed metric.

absences: No procurement records or channel-mix data from any specific large operator (hyperscaler, colo, neocloud) quantifying the share of 2026-27 PDU spend that is a discrete, operator-selected direct-purchase line versus embedded in integrator/incumbent rack deals.

No breakdown, percentage, or dollar figure isolating PDU value within any of the found bundled contracts (Jülich tender, Foxconn-Schneider partnership, Iren-Dell deal) — PDUs appear only as line-item mentions inside larger IT/rack packages, never separately priced or channel-attributed.
No interview, survey, or filing-based data on how many large operators tender PDUs independently of their server/rack/integrator contracts for AI-density halls specifically.
The search surfaced only forward-looking partnership announcements and single-instance tender documents, not a cross-operator comparison (2-3 operators) that the intent specifically requested.
